summary: Plan-mode PR inventory classification for 10 listed refs. Security-sensitive PRs are scoped to route_security. One already-closed candidate is kept closed. One open disk-space PR is safe to close as superseded by the already-merged hydrated fix PR #90247. Remaining non-security PRs stay open for maintainer review or normal PR handling.

actions.reason: Workspace alias filesystem boundary changes are out of scope for backlog cleanup automation and should go to central OpenClaw security handling.
Broad native meta-skill orchestration changes touch tool/runtime surfaces and security-shaped dependency review, so this item should be quarantined to central OpenClaw security handling.
This is a standalone provider-fix PR with failed proof and no safe closure target in this inventory shard.
Token handoff/auth-provider changes cross an authentication/security boundary and should be handled by central OpenClaw security review.
This PR is related to the latency feature thread but is only a partial server-side step and should remain open for normal maintainer review.
The same root cause and file-level fix already landed via #90247, leaving no unique remaining work in #90530.
Already closed refs must not receive close actions; historical evidence points to #91566 as the landed replacement path.
This is a standalone channel compatibility fix PR that should remain open for maintainer review rather than be closed in this inventory pass.
Runtime tool allowlist behavior touches an exec-adjacent security boundary in this artifact and should be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
This is a plausible linked fix path for #58838 but needs normal PR maintenance and maintainer review, not closure or merge planning in this shard.
